claims.claim_text: The opening airline anecdote gives a dated, specific chatbot-failure incident framing that does not match the supportable public record as written.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters | first sentence
claims.anchor_text: In November 2023, a prominent North American airline faced a highly publicized legal and public relations disaster when their customer-facing AI chatbot confidently invented a bereavement fare policy.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A customer-service chatbot at a major airline gave incorrect information about bereavement fares, triggering a legal dispute and public embarrassment.
claims.rationale: The original sentence mixes false or unsupported specifics into a real incident and should be collapsed to the verified operational lesson.

claims.claim_text: The module claims the airline froze its entire generative-AI strategy and wrote off millions in engineering investment.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters | first paragraph, financial-impact sentence
claims.anchor_text: The financial impact extended far beyond the single ticket refund; the airline suffered intense reputational damage, froze their entire generative AI deployment strategy, and had to write off millions of dollars in engineering investment.
claims.suggested_rewrite: The fallout extended beyond the refund itself and highlighted how chatbot mistakes can create reputational and program-level consequences.
claims.rationale: Those financial and program-level specifics are not supported by an allowlisted source here.

claims.claim_text: RAG augments generation with explicit non-parametric memory retrieved at runtime.
claims.span_hint: section: The Heureka Moment | non-parametric memory discussion
claims.rationale: The RAG paper explicitly describes retrieval-augmented generation as combining parametric and non-parametric memory.

claims.claim_text: LoRA freezes pretrained weights and injects trainable low-rank matrices, reducing trainable parameters relative to full fine-tuning.
claims.span_hint: section: Theory: The Two Approaches to Customizing LLMs | Fine-tuning and PEFT discussion
claims.rationale: The LoRA paper directly states that LoRA freezes pretrained weights and adds trainable rank-decomposition matrices.

claims.claim_text: `text-embedding-3-large` is an OpenAI embedding model used for retrieval-style applications.
claims.span_hint: section: Theory: The Two Approaches to Customizing LLMs | RAG mechanism paragraph
claims.rationale: OpenAI's product announcement directly introduces `text-embedding-3-large` and explains its retrieval-oriented use.

claims.claim_text: The monthly RAG cost table is presented as concrete pricing rather than an illustrative example.
claims.span_hint: section: The Cost Reality | RAG Costs (Monthly)
claims.rationale: These costs depend heavily on current provider pricing, token mix, and architecture choices, so they should be framed as illustrative.

claims.claim_text: The fine-tuning cost table is presented as concrete pricing rather than an illustrative example.
claims.span_hint: section: The Cost Reality | Fine-tuning Costs (One-time + Ongoing)
claims.rationale: Upfront training, evaluation, and hosting costs are highly assumption-sensitive and should not be presented as fixed quotes.

claims.claim_text: The hidden-cost timeline is presented as a fixed duration estimate rather than an illustrative planning sketch.
claims.span_hint: section: The Cost Reality | The Hidden Costs of Fine-Tuning
claims.suggested_rewrite: ### The Hidden Costs of Fine-Tuning (Illustrative Timeline)
claims.rationale: Data-pipeline and evaluation work are real, but the exact week counts are team- and workflow-dependent.

claims.claim_text: The two-year TCO comparison is presented as a definitive cost disparity rather than an illustrative scenario.
claims.span_hint: section: The Cost Reality | sentence before two-year horizon table
claims.anchor_text: When accounting for engineering salaries over a two-year horizon, the disparity becomes shocking:
claims.suggested_rewrite: Over a two-year horizon, staffing, hosting, and iteration assumptions can swing the totals substantially, so treat the following comparison as illustrative:
claims.rationale: Multi-year TCO comparisons are too assumption-heavy to present as universal facts without a pinned methodology.

claims.claim_text: The one-million-query cost breakdown is presented as a concrete pricing scenario rather than an illustrative example.
claims.span_hint: section: The Cost Reality | Scenario Breakdown: 1 Million Queries per Month
claims.anchor_text: ### Scenario Breakdown: 1 Million Queries per Month
claims.suggested_rewrite: ### Illustrative Scenario Breakdown: 1 Million Queries per Month
claims.rationale: The token and hosting math here depends on current model prices and workload assumptions and should be framed as illustrative.

claims.claim_text: The latency section presents exact end-to-end numbers as typical production values.
claims.span_hint: section: The Latency Trade-off: When Milliseconds Matter
claims.anchor_text: ## The Latency Trade-off: When Milliseconds Matter
claims.suggested_rewrite: ## The Latency Trade-off: Illustrative Latency Budget
claims.rationale: Exact latency budgets vary widely by model, network path, caching, and retrieval topology.

claims.claim_text: Typical vector-only retrieval accuracy hovers around 70-85% in production.
claims.span_hint: section: The RAG Reliability Problem (And How to Solve It)
claims.anchor_text: Typical vector-only retrieval accuracy hovers around 70-85% in production.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Vector-only retrieval often misses relevant context in production, which is why teams add hybrid retrieval and reranking.
claims.rationale: This exact production accuracy range needs a pinned source and should not remain as an uncited number.

claims.claim_text: The module claims an unattributed fintech deployment lost $180,000 because of stale fine-tuned pricing answers.
claims.span_hint: section: When Fine-tuning Goes Wrong | Horror Story 1
claims.anchor_text: The business lost $180,000 honoring the hallucinated fees before pulling the deployment.
claims.suggested_rewrite: The business incurred real costs from honoring incorrect answers before the deployment was pulled.
claims.rationale: This is a fake-precise loss figure attached to an unattributed anecdote.

claims.claim_text: The module presents an unattributed story about combining five client voices into one fine-tuned model.
claims.span_hint: section: When Fine-tuning Goes Wrong | Horror Story 2
claims.anchor_text: A marketing agency attempted to consolidate five distinct client brand voices into a single fine-tuned model.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Trying to force several incompatible brand voices into one fine-tuned model can produce unstable mixed-style outputs.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed production anecdote with fake-precise scope.

claims.claim_text: The module claims an unattributed full fine-tune caused benchmark scores to drop by over 40%.
claims.span_hint: section: When Fine-tuning Goes Wrong | Horror Story 3
claims.anchor_text: The model entirely lost its ability to perform basic logical reasoning and arithmetic, dropping its benchmark scores by over 40%.
claims.suggested_rewrite: The model lost important general-purpose reasoning ability after the overly narrow full fine-tune.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed anecdote with a precise benchmark impact that is not sourced.

claims.claim_text: QLoRA backpropagates through a frozen 4-bit quantized base model into LoRA adapters, and the paper demonstrates fine-tuning a 65B model on a single 48GB GPU.
claims.span_hint: section: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) | Quantized LoRA (QLoRA)
claims.rationale: The QLoRA abstract directly states the frozen 4-bit setup and the single-48GB-GPU 65B result.

claims.claim_text: The module claims QLoRA enables fine-tuning 70B models on commodity GPU hardware.
claims.span_hint: section: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) | Quantized LoRA (QLoRA)
claims.anchor_text: This engineering feat allows developers to fine-tune massive 70-billion parameter models on commodity GPU hardware.
claims.suggested_rewrite: This engineering feat dramatically lowers the hardware required for fine-tuning very large models compared with full-precision full fine-tuning.
claims.rationale: The exact 70B-on-commodity-hardware claim overreaches the sourceable paper result.

claims.claim_text: A 2023 MLOps Community survey found that 67% of production fine-tuning projects were abandoned within 18 months.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know? | first bullet
claims.anchor_text: - **Did You Know?** In a 2023 survey by the MLOps Community, 67% of production fine-tuning projects were abandoned within 18 months because rapidly advancing base models entirely outpaced their expensive, custom-trained counterparts.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **Did You Know?** Rapidly improving base models can erase the ROI case for some expensive fine-tuning projects.
claims.rationale: This exact survey statistic needs a pinned primary source that is not provided here.

claims.claim_text: Catastrophic forgetting was first formally documented in 1989 by McCloskey and Cohen.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know? | second bullet
claims.anchor_text: - **Did You Know?** Catastrophic forgetting in neural networks was first formally documented in 1989 by researchers McCloskey and Cohen, a fundamental problem that still plagues massive LLMs today when full fine-tuning is applied without heavy regularization.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **Did You Know?** Catastrophic forgetting has been studied for decades and remains relevant when aggressive fine-tuning overwrites broad base-model capabilities.
claims.rationale: The dated attribution is plausible but too specific for this module without a direct source.

claims.claim_text: Uber's production RAG system processes roughly 2 billion embedding updates per day and cut daily compute from $200,000 to $8,000.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know? | third bullet
claims.anchor_text: - **Did You Know?** Uber's production RAG system processes roughly 2 billion embedding updates every single day, utilizing custom incremental indexing to reduce their daily compute bill from $200,000 to an astoundingly low $8,000.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **Did You Know?** Large production RAG systems often invest heavily in incremental indexing and retrieval infrastructure to control refresh costs.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed company anecdote with multiple fake-precise operational numbers.

claims.claim_text: The module claims the LoRA paper showed effective fine-tuning of a 7B model while updating just 0.8% of weights.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know? | fourth bullet
claims.anchor_text: - **Did You Know?** The seminal LoRA paper by Hu et al., published in June 2021, empirically demonstrated that fine-tuning a 7-billion parameter model could be done effectively while updating as little as 0.8% of its original weights, democratizing model customization forever.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - **Did You Know?** The seminal LoRA paper showed that low-rank adapters can preserve strong downstream performance while training far fewer parameters than full fine-tuning.
claims.rationale: The named paper is real, but this exact 7B and 0.8% phrasing is too specific for the module as written.

further_reading.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary source for the parametric-versus-non-parametric memory framing behind modern RAG.

further_reading.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary source for why LoRA changes behavior with far fewer trainable parameters than full fine-tuning.

further_reading.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
further_reading.why_relevant: Best source for the 4-bit quantized-adapter workflow and the realistic hardware implications of QLoRA.

notes: No existing vector-rag citation pool was present. Most citation-required problems in this module are unsupported pricing sketches and unattributed war stories, so the seed leans heavily on rewrite dispositions.
